run_summary: Portfolio is flat cash at $10.3k. Track record shows 60% hit rate (3/5), with OVERDONE calls at 60% hit rate and confidence-3 calls at 50%. This suggests moderate calibration across all confidence levels. Reviewing 18 new flags across earnings season: most are correctly classified as RATIONAL or PARTIALLY RATIONAL (dominated by post-earnings reactions and sector sympathy). Three OVERDONE candidates (AEIS, and two UNCLEAR/PARTIALLY names warrant consideration), two UNCLEAR momentum plays (AAON, MIDD), and one strong CONVICTION candidate (PLNT short, but we don't short in this framework). I'm identifying 2–3 buys: one exploratory (AEIS as a sympathy fade on solid 8-K reading), one conviction (PLNT as a clear structural break if I could short—instead I'll skip), and one exploratory (AAON on data-center asymmetry).

entries.name: Shake Shack, Inc.
entries.thesis: Two 8-Ks filed 5/7: one with 2.02 earnings, the other with 5.02 officer departure. The combination of an earnings print AND a management change in the same filing window is a textbook bad-news bundle. With the stock already down 32.5% over 5 days, today's -8% likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news. Magnitude appears justified given dual catalysts.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be planned board succession or a controlled CEO transition with a strong replacement named — then the -32% 5-day move is overdone and a bounce candidate.
entries.reasoning: Shake Shack is classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4) with dual catalysts (earnings + 5.02 officer departure in same window) justifying a -32.5% 5-day collapse and -8% today. The thesis is sound—companies rarely volunteer two negative events unless forced. My track record on RATIONAL classifications is not yet established (only 1 resolved call in the OVERDONE bucket), but this feels like a fairly-priced drawdown to a new equilibrium. Adding here would be catching a falling knife without a clear reversal catalyst. Skip for now; revisit if the 5.02 turns out to be planned succession or if guidance holds.

entries.name: Whirlpool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Post-earnings selloff + April home sales disappointment
entries.thesis: WHR reported 5/6 (2.02). Stock down 22.6% over 5 days and another -8.6% today — today's leg likely tied to the April home sales miss reported this morning (CNBC). Appliances are tightly correlated to existing-home turnover. Compounding catalyst, magnitude looks justified.
entries.what_kills: Mortgage rates pull back sharply on Fed/Iran resolution; April housing data revised; WHR announces meaningful tariff offset or restructuring.
entries.reasoning: Whirlpool is down 22.6% over 5 days + 8.6% today on post-earnings + April housing miss (CNBC). Appliance demand is tightly tied to existing-home turnover; the dual catalyst justifies magnitude. This is classic LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4) and I see no misprice. Housing data is a rolling tape over coming weeks—no immediate catalyst to mean-revert the stock. Revisit if mortgage rates pull back sharply or housing data revises higher.

entries.name: Installed Building Products
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7 with 2.02. Stock now down 25.6% over 5 days. Today's -6% likely pure housing-data sympathy (April home sales miss). The cumulative move is large for what is typically a high-quality compounder; magnitude reflects builder slowdown but watch for overshoot if housing data stabilizes. Worth a closer look than SHAK or WHR because IBP has a stronger historical track record.
entries.what_kills: Earnings actually beat and selloff is purely sector-driven; insider buying emerges; M&A pipeline commentary remains strong.
entries.reasoning: Installed Building Products is down 25.6% over 5 days on Q1 earnings + housing sympathy. Classified PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3) and the thesis is sound—cumulative move is large but justified by builder slowdown. However, IBP is noted as a 'high-quality compounder' with strong historical track record, and the suggestion is to wait 5–10 trading days post-print for a cleaner entry with more granular segment data. The setup is interesting (quality name hammered on cyclical data), but today's action is not yet a clear fade signal. Watch for stabilization and upcoming builder commentary from DHI/LEN/PHM.

entries.name: Hecla Mining
entries.thesis: CNBC notes silver surged 7% to highest since March on AI/parabolic rally narrative. Hecla is the largest US primary silver producer — operating leverage on a 7% silver move easily justifies +11%. Earnings filed 5/5 likely already digested. This is rational commodity beta with a recent positive print as the floor.
entries.what_kills: Silver futures reverse on profit-taking; HL 5-day gain is already +17.6%, so positioning getting crowded.
entries.reasoning: Hecla Mining is up 11% on silver surge (+7% to multi-month high) and Q1 earnings. This is LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4)—operating leverage on a 7% silver move is textbook. However, the 5-day gain is already +17.6%, which suggests positioning is crowded. No mean-reversion catalyst is apparent; this is a commodity momentum play that benefits from sustained silver strength, not from an earnings surprise or a fundamental repricing. Skip; let momentum exhaust before considering.

entries.name: Celsius Holdings
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. -8.7% over 5 days with another -5% today, on 2.26x volume. Likely combination of post-earnings drift and consumer staples weakness (XLP and ELF also down). Magnitude consistent with growth-name multiple compression on any whiff of distribution slowdown.
entries.what_kills: Scanner data shows reaccel; PEP reaffirms support; Alani Nu integration commentary is positive.
entries.reasoning: Celsius is down 13.7% over 5 days (including -5% today) on Q1 earnings + staples sector weakness (XLP -0.96%). The thesis is PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3)—post-earnings drift + growth-name multiple compression are typical. However, there is no clear catalyst for mean reversion in the near term; Nielsen scanner data and distribution health will be the key reads, and those roll gradually. No urgent entry point. Revisit if XLP stabilizes or if Celsius shows distribution strength in upcoming weeks.

entries.name: Insight Enterprises
entries.thesis: Reported 5/7. 5-day +25.2% — print was clearly a beat. Today's +5% is continuation, plausibly tied to enterprise AI adoption narrative (OpenAI Dresser commentary today). NSIT is an IT solutions/reseller play with direct AI infrastructure exposure. Magnitude rational but momentum is extended.
entries.what_kills: Extension into low-float momentum chase fades; gross margin in transcript turns out worse than headline EPS.
entries.reasoning: Insight Enterprises is up 5% today on a 25% 5-day rally following Q1 earnings. The beat is real and the AI narrative is intact (OpenAI commentary today), but momentum is extended. Classified PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3) and today's action is just continuation. Without a fresh catalyst (analyst upside revision, CDW/ARW surge), the setup is extended-momentum not mean-reversion. Skip; revisit if there's a pullback and a follow-up catalyst emerges.

entries.name: Kodiak Gas Services
entries.thesis: Earnings filed today (5/11). Stock +8.4% on 2x volume into a strong energy tape (Hormuz commentary, oil concerns). Combination of an in-the-day 2.02 print plus +XLE makes the magnitude reasonable. 5-day +10.9% suggests pre-positioning was modest, so post-print rally is real reaction.
entries.what_kills: Energy unwind on Iran ceasefire revival; secondary equity offering announcement.
entries.reasoning: Kodiak Gas is up 8.4% on earnings + XLE tailwind (+2.64%), classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4). The print looks solid and the energy tape is supportive, but there's no oversold setup to revert—this is a momentum continuation. If Hormuz tension eases or oil unwinds, KGS will follow the sector down. Magnitude is justified; no edge to chase. Skip.

entries.name: Hawaiian Electric
entries.thesis: Reported 5/8 (2.02). -6% today on 2.76x volume despite XLU +0.94% — meaningful underperformance vs sector. HE still carries Maui wildfire litigation overhang; earnings likely surfaced reserve/liability commentary that re-rates the credit. Magnitude justified if filing has updated reserves disclosure.
entries.what_kills: Reaction is purely a soft EPS print with no incremental litigation news — bounces in days.
entries.reasoning: Hawaiian Electric is down 6% on Q1 earnings despite XLU +0.94%, suggesting underperformance driven by wildfire litigation-reserve commentary. PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3). The liability overhang is structural and will persist until litigation clarity emerges. Today's move is likely justified; no tactical entry point. Revisit if litigation headlines resolve or if balance-sheet capacity for new reserves is disclosed positively.

entries.name: Fluor Corporation
entries.thesis: Fluor is down 15% on Q1 print; CNBC framed it as a pullback opportunity, suggesting the print wasn't a thesis-breaker but rather a guide/timing miss typical of E&C. Five-day -18% means most of the damage is already in. Engineering & construction earnings frequently swing on project timing and one-offs (NuScale stake mark, segment margins) rather than secular issues. The 8-K is just 2.02 + 5.07 (annual meeting vote) — no impairment, no restructuring, no management change.
entries.what_kills: Backlog declining, large project write-downs disclosed in Q, or segment margin guide cut materially — would justify the magnitude and more.
entries.reasoning: Fluor is down 15% on Q1 print but the 8-K contains only 2.02 + 5.07 (no impairment, no restructuring, no management change). Classified PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3). The CNBC framing suggests the print was not a thesis-breaker but a timing/mix miss. The 5-day -18% implies most damage is in. The setup is interesting IF the transcript shows backlog growth intact and the miss is tied to NuScale equity mark or project timing. However, I need to see the actual transcript read-through before committing. Watch for granular detail on project margins and segment health.

entries.name: Oshkosh Corporation
entries.thesis: OSK -9.6% on Q1 print with only 2.02 + 5.07 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change). Coming on the same day as Trump's renewed EU tariff threats, an industrial with European exposure and access-equipment cyclicality is a natural target. Cumulative -10.9% over 5 days. Without granular guide info, hard to say if 9.6% is calibrated, but absence of structural codes suggests it's a guide/margin issue rather than a thesis-break.
entries.what_kills: Backlog reaffirmed, NGDV on track, miss tied to Q1 mix only — magnitude becomes harder to defend.
entries.reasoning: Oshkosh is down 9.6% on Q1 earnings amid Trump tariff-threat tape. The 8-K is 2.02 + 5.07 (no impairment, no management change). Classified PARTIALLY RATIONAL (conf 3). Without granular guide info, magnitude is uncertain. However, the tariff tape is macro-momentum driven and will persist if trade tension stays elevated. No clear catalyst to fade from current levels. Skip; revisit if tariff rhetoric softens or if transcript shows NGDV backlog resilience.

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + likely capital return announcement
entries.thesis: TXRH +12% on print with 2.02 + 8.01 8-K (8.01 likely a dividend/buyback announcement based on past pattern). TXRH is a quality compounder that has been pressured on consumer/inflation fears; a clear comps beat plus capital return action would justify a 10%+ rerate, especially as a relative-strength rotation candidate vs. discretionary peers. Volume 2.45x supports real institutional buying. Inspire Brands IPO filing today reinforces restaurant-sector attention.
entries.what_kills: Beat is purely pricing-driven with negative traffic; margin guide softens for H2.
entries.reasoning: Texas Roadhouse is up 12% on Q1 earnings beat in a quality compounder. Classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4) with 2.02 + 8.01 (likely capital return). The pairing signals confidence and a comps beat in a defensive name is meaningful. Volume 2.45x supports institutional buying, and the Inspire Brands IPO filing reinforces restaurant-sector attention. This is a fairly-priced re-rating, not an overshoot to fade. The setup is strong for TXRH holders but no entry signal for new longs. Skip.

entries.name: Kennametal
entries.thesis: KMT is a tooling/cutting bellwether tied to manufacturing PMI and energy capex. -12.6% likely reflects guide cut on weak GE Aerospace tooling demand or oil & gas slowdown despite XLE relative stability. Read-through to broader short-cycle industrials.
entries.what_kills: If miss is pure FX or one-time charge with end-markets stable, sets up bounce
entries.reasoning: Kennametal is down 12.6% on Q3 FY26 earnings, likely reflecting weak GE Aerospace tooling demand or oil & gas slowdown. Classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 3). The read-through to short-cycle industrial weakness is real; no tactical oversold setup. Skip unless XLE unwinds sharply and signals a broader energy correction that KMT could participate in.

entries.name: AAON, Inc.
entries.thesis: A 31% single-day move on earnings + 8.01 'other events' often signals more than just a beat — possibilities include a major data-center/AI cooling contract win, raised guide, or strategic announcement bundled with the print. AAON's exposure to data-center liquid cooling makes a contract-win narrative highly plausible given the AI capex tape.
entries.what_kills: 8.01 turns out to be routine (dividend, buyback) and the move is purely a short squeeze on a guide-raise — in that case 31% is hard to sustain without follow-through fundamentals.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The bull thesis hinges on the unread 8.01 containing 'asymmetric news' (contract or guide raise), but a 31% move on earnings alone in a small-cap industrial is explained by short covering and does not require material business news to justify. Bear case: AAON is a $1.5B market-cap stock with a historically volatile shareholder base and small float; a 31% single-day move is within the range for a beat on sentiment alone, without requiring a named hyperscaler contract or raised guide. The bull case explicitly acknowledges the move could be 'purely a short squeeze on a guide-raise,' which is a euphemism for momentum-driven repricing that has no fundamental anchor. The 8.01 is unread and the reasoning that 'data-center liquid cooling exposure makes a contract plausible' is narrative backfill, not forward-looking evidence. Until the 8.01 exhibit is filed and confirms a specific contract or material guide change, this is a classic post-earnings momentum trap where the 31% move front-runs news that may not materialize. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: AAON is up 31% on Q1 earnings + 8.01 'other events' bundled in a single 8-K. Classified UNCLEAR (conf 3). A 31% single-day move on earnings often signals more than a beat—possibilities include a major hyperscaler cooling contract (plausible given AAON's data-center liquid cooling exposure), a raised guide, or a strategic announcement. The 8.01 is the asymmetric piece; if it discloses a named contract or material guide raise, the move is justified and momentum could persist. If the 8.01 is just routine (dividend/buyback), the move is a short squeeze and hard to sustain. A 6% exploratory position lets me test the thesis: does the 8.01 contain asymmetric news (contract/guide raise) or just routine? If contract-related, AAON could re-rate higher; if routine, the move unwinds. Catalyst is immediate (press release or exhibit filing).)

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis hinges on the unread 8.01 containing 'asymmetric news' (contract or guide raise), but a 31% move on earnings alone in a small-cap industrial is explained by short covering and does not require material business news to justify.
red_team.critique: AAON is a $1.5B market-cap stock with a historically volatile shareholder base and small float; a 31% single-day move is within the range for a beat on sentiment alone, without requiring a named hyperscaler contract or raised guide. The bull case explicitly acknowledges the move could be 'purely a short squeeze on a guide-raise,' which is a euphemism for momentum-driven repricing that has no fundamental anchor. The 8.01 is unread and the reasoning that 'data-center liquid cooling exposure makes a contract plausible' is narrative backfill, not forward-looking evidence. Until the 8.01 exhibit is filed and confirms a specific contract or material guide change, this is a classic post-earnings momentum trap where the 31% move front-runs news that may not materialize.

entries.name: Planet Fitness
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings + guide cut + canceled price hike
entries.thesis: Slashing guidance AND canceling a planned price increase is a double-negative: it confirms both demand softness and management's loss of confidence in pricing power. That combination justifies severe re-rating in a unit-growth franchise model where pricing was a key thesis component. 31% is harsh but mechanism is clearly identified.
entries.what_kills: Management frames cancelation as goodwill/timing rather than demand; member counts actually growing — would suggest overreaction.
entries.reasoning: Planet Fitness is down 31% on guide cut + canceled price-hike announcement. Classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 4). This is a structural pricing-power break—when a growth company cancels a previously-announced price increase AND cuts guidance simultaneously, the market correctly re-rates it as loss of pricing leverage in a unit-growth franchise model. The magnitude is harsh but justified. This would be a compelling short in a portfolio that permits shorting, but in a long-only framework, there's no edge here. The downside case is fully priced. Skip.

entries.name: Paycom Software
entries.thesis: PAYC has been a heavily-shorted disappointer over the last two years. A 10% rally on earnings with moderate volume in a risk-off tape is a healthy beat — possibly with stabilization in Beti automation cannibalization narrative that has plagued the stock. Counter-tape strength is meaningful.
entries.what_kills: Beat was driven by float income or one-time items rather than core SaaS growth.
entries.reasoning: Paycom is up 9.5% on Q1 earnings beat in a beaten-down HCM name that has been a heavy short target. Classified LIKELY RATIONAL (conf 3). The move is moderate and counter-tape (risk-off environment), which is healthy. However, the thesis piece here is whether the Beti automation cannibalization narrative is stabilizing—this requires seeing translator commentary on product mix and sales productivity metrics. The setup is interesting (heavily-shorted name showing relative strength) but I need more detail on the stabilization thesis before committing. Watch for ADP/PCTY commentary and upcoming HCM competitive data.

entries.name: Middleby Corporation
entries.thesis: MIDD filed a 7.01 Reg FD on May 4 — three days before the earnings 8-K — which often precedes strategic announcements (spin-off update, segment sale). Combined with the 11% rally, possible the company advanced a previously-discussed Food Processing or Residential spin-off plan.
entries.what_kills: May 4 8-K turns out to be investor day deck only, with no spin commitment.
entries.reasoning: Middleby is up 11% on Q1 earnings + a prior 7.01 Reg FD filed May 4 (three days before the print). Classified UNCLEAR (conf 3). The sequencing is notable—a Reg FD that precedes earnings often telegraphs strategic content (spin-off update, segment sale). The possibility of a Food Processing or Residential spin-off is asymmetric if disclosed. However, the May 4 Reg FD could be just an investor day deck with no spin commitment. I need to read the actual May 4 and May 7 filings to confirm. Watch; if the Reg FD or earnings commentary confirms a spin-off structure/timeline, this becomes a BUY candidate.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no visible filings or earnings catalyst
entries.thesis: No 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings flagged, volume actually below average — a 6%+ drop on 0.7x volume is unusual and suggests either (a) a single seller / illiquidity, (b) sympathy with a peer guide-down not in our feed, or (c) something specific not yet showing in filings. Cannot articulate a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: If price recovers tomorrow on no news, confirms today was thin-tape noise; if an 8-K appears overnight, the catalyst was simply not yet filed when we looked.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.thesis: PLAB -5.4% with SMH -2.6% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap semi-supply-chain name on a sharp semi down day. Slightly elevated volume (1.25x) but no catalyst_signals — most likely pure sector sympathy. Magnitude is in line with what high-beta semi small caps typically do on a 2.5%+ SMH down move.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a PLAB-specific catalyst (customer concentration warning, China export-control news given Trump's China trip context) would reclassify this as something other than rational sympathy.

entries.name: Vicor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possibly AI-power sympathy or unfiled customer-related news
entries.thesis: VICR is a power-management chip supplier with AI datacenter exposure (48V power delivery for GPU racks). +22% on no SEC filing and modest 2x volume looks like either a private news leak (customer win, design-in disclosure at a conference) or aggressive AI-power sympathy buying piggybacking on NVDA/SMH strength. The 5-day change of only 24.7% means almost all of this is today.
entries.what_kills: No follow-on news within 48 hours and the move fades back below $290 — suggests a single-day momentum/short-squeeze with no fundamental driver. Empty 8-K slate at this magnitude is suspicious.

entries.name: Dropbox
entries.thesis: DBX reported 5/7 and 5-day change is +5.6% — meaning the stock initially rallied on earnings, but today is giving back -8.3%. This pattern (rally then fade) often indicates either sell-side downgrade following deeper-dive, or a guidance footnote investors initially missed. Worth investigating because the inversion of initial reaction is unusual.
entries.what_kills: Move is just broad SaaS weakness sympathy with no DBX-specific re-read; bounces back tomorrow.

entries.name: e.l.f. Beauty
entries.thesis: ELF reports in 9 days. -9% on 2.5x volume with no 8-K is pre-earnings de-risking, possibly tied to weakening Nielsen/Circana data in beauty or sympathy with broader consumer weakness. Magnitude is large for pre-positioning unless someone has a channel-check note out today.
entries.what_kills: Buyers step in pre-print; sector rebounds; no specific channel-check note surfaces.

entries.name: Ralliant Corporation
entries.thesis: RAL reports 5/12 pre-market — tomorrow morning. +4.9% on near-3x volume into a print is aggressive positioning. 5-day +8.8%. Either leaked positive whisper or sympathy with semi/electronic-components strength (SMH +1.7%, VICR +22%). Asymmetric setup: positioning into a print at near 52-week extension is a fade candidate if guide is anything less than blowout.
entries.what_kills: In-line print causes immediate -5% gap; or VICR-style rally is just a sympathy halo that fades.

entries.name: Gap, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Possibly tariff/apparel concerns or unknown channel data
entries.thesis: GAP -7% on 2x volume with empty catalyst_signals. Could be tariff-related (Trump tariff Truth Social posts, apparel imports), pre-earnings de-risking (typically reports late May), or sympathy with other retail weakness. Without a specific catalyst, magnitude is hard to validate.
entries.what_kills: No follow-on news, bounces tomorrow — was just a positioning trade.

entries.name: BILL Holdings, Inc.
entries.thesis: BILL is up 11% on a print that includes a 2.05 (exit/disposal costs) item. Normally 2.05 is a drag, but when paired with a positive stock reaction it usually means the market is rewarding the cost-cut/right-sizing story over the charge itself — classic 'kitchen sink + better core' setup. BILL has been a battleground/short name; a restructuring narrative could be re-rating fuel. Risk is the rally fades once people parse the charge magnitude vs. forward op-margin guide.
entries.what_kills: Restructuring charge is large with no offsetting margin commitment; revenue guide cut. In that case the 11% pop fades.

entries.name: Globus Medical, Inc.
entries.thesis: GMED is down 8% on the print after already being down 14.7% over 5 days, suggesting the market knew something was coming and the report confirmed it. With only 2.02 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change), the driver is likely guide cut or competitive concerns (NuVasive integration, robotics share). The cumulative 23% drawdown in a defensive medtech is more interesting than today's 8% in isolation — could be approaching washout or could mark a structural break.
entries.what_kills: Print shows revenue beat with one-time margin pressure; would suggest forced-seller dynamics and a likely bounce.

entries.name: MaxLinear, Inc.
entries.thesis: MXL +21% with NO 8-K and only 1.16x volume is a tell — this is a pure sympathy move on the AMD/Intel/Micron memory/CPU rotation, possibly amplified by short covering (MXL has been a battleground name with prior infrastructure overhang). The low volume multiple is the key warning: a 21% move on barely-elevated volume = thin-tape squeeze, not real institutional accumulation. 5-day +29% means it's already extended.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays low into next session and price reverses — confirms squeeze-not-reposition. Also: an analyst note pointing out MXL has minimal direct exposure to the memory/CPU theme would be damaging.

entries.name: Penguin Solutions, Inc.
entries.thesis: PENG (formerly SMART Global) is a memory/storage solutions company — direct beneficiary of the memory chip rally narrative covered in CNBC. No 8-K, but 5-day +41% indicates the market has been actively repricing it on the Micron-led memory shortage thesis. Today's 13.5% is continuation, not initiation. Risk: it's already extended and may be late-stage in this rotation.
entries.what_kills: Memory rally rolls over (Micron tops out); PENG retraces faster than peers given its smaller size.

entries.name: LifeStance Health Group
entries.thesis: LFST -13% with 2.02 + 7.01 + 9.01. The 7.01 (Reg FD) is what's interesting — when paired with earnings it typically contains either a strategic update, guide change, or M&A-related disclosure. For a roll-up like LFST that's been struggling with clinician retention and unit economics, the addition of FD content suggests something material beyond the print. Magnitude is large but not extreme for this name's history of volatility.
entries.what_kills: FD disclosure is a positive strategic update (e.g., new payer contract) that the market mis-read, suggesting a snap-back is possible.

entries.name: ESCO Technologies
entries.thesis: Volume multiple only 1.45x is unusually low for a -9% drop, suggesting a high-quality holder base just trimming on a guide that was reset rather than panic. ESE typically trades at premium multiple; any miss compresses materially. Not a clear opportunity but watch for stabilization.
entries.what_kills: If the print is actually fine and selling is just multiple compression on an expensive name, it could continue lower without news

entries.name: Assured Guaranty
entries.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + next-day Reg FD disclosure
entries.thesis: Two filings in two days (earnings 5/7, then 7.01 Reg FD on 5/8) plus a -9.75% drop is unusual for a financial guarantee insurer that typically grinds. The follow-up 7.01 the day after earnings often signals clarification or correction of guidance — worth digging into the FD disclosure.
entries.what_kills: If 7.01 is benign (investor day slides, dividend reaffirmation), -9.75% is overdone for a defensive insurer

entries.name: H&R Block
entries.thesis: HRB's fiscal Q3 captures the bulk of tax-season revenue. A 24% gap typically requires both a beat and a guide raise or capital-return announcement. Counter-trend to broader consumer cyclical weakness suggests company-specific positive catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Beat is purely a one-time tax-prep volume spike with no margin/buyback follow-through.

entries.name: Coca-Cola Consolidated
entries.thesis: COKE has rallied massively over recent years on margin expansion thesis; a 16% gap on a bottler is unusual and likely reflects margin compression (PET, aluminum, freight) plus volume softness. Low-float dynamics can amplify; possible overshoot but bottler margin reversals tend to persist multiple quarters.
entries.what_kills: One-time hurricane/weather impact with margin recovery in Q2 commentary.

entries.name: Lumen Technologies
entries.thesis: Lumen reported May 5; today's additional 8.01 'other events' filing combined with continued slide suggests follow-on disclosure (debt action, customer loss, AI-connectivity contract update). Volume only 1.29x average is notable — selling pressure isn't massive, suggesting drift rather than capitulation.
entries.what_kills: 8.01 is benign (annual meeting, routine matter) — then move is sympathy with telco/leveraged names.

entries.name: DXP Enterprises
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
entries.thesis: Empty catalyst_signals and only 1.65x volume on a 17% drop is unusual — could be a delayed earnings reaction, a private placement leak, or an industrial-distribution sympathy move (with XLI -1.6% and oil/energy tape weak, DXPE's energy-services exposure may be the driver). Without a filing or news, call this honestly: don't know.
entries.what_kills: Stock recovers on no news — confirms it was thin-trading dislocation.

entries.name: Primoris Services Corporation
entries.thesis: Earnings + officer departure (5.02) + 'other events' combined — magnitude suggests guidance cut, project writedown, or CFO/CEO exit on top of miss. A 50% single-day drop in an engineering & construction name with $5.5B cap implies the market is pricing material backlog/contract impairment, not just a quarterly miss.
entries.what_kills: If 8-K shows planned CEO retirement with successor named and earnings only modestly missed, then -50% is overdone. If it shows restatement risk or project impairment of major scale, then move is justified or even insufficient.

entries.name: TransMedics Group
entries.thesis: Clean earnings 8-K only (2.02 + 9.01) — no officer departure or restructuring flagged. -23% on an earnings print with no other red-flag items typically means revenue miss or guide-down on growth deceleration (TMDX has historically traded at growth multiples on OCS organ transport adoption).
entries.what_kills: If guide was reaffirmed and miss was modest, -23% is overdone and likely a setup for bounce. If full-year guide cut materially or aviation segment showed structural problems, move is justified.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments
entries.thesis: Semi-equipment beat into a tape where SMH +5.2% and TSM +6.4% are confirming AI-capex acceleration. Veeco leverages advanced packaging (laser annealing, MOCVD) — direct beneficiary of TSM/leading-edge capex commentary.
entries.what_kills: If guide was in-line and move is purely sympathy/short-cover, fade risk after sector cools

entries.name: Axcelis Technologies
entries.catalyst: Sector sympathy + pre-earnings positioning (reports today after close)
entries.thesis: ACLS reports AFTER hours TODAY (in ~10 hours) — this is pre-positioning into earnings inside a +5% semi-equipment tape. 5.07 8-K is just shareholder vote results, not catalyst. The 22% pre-earnings rip is mostly sympathy with VECO/SMH and short squeeze.
entries.what_kills: Strong beat-and-raise tonight justifies the move; in-line print likely fades 5-10% as the sympathy premium unwinds

entries.name: Kyndryl Holdings
entries.thesis: 8-K has 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) alongside earnings — explicit restructuring charge announcement. -11% reasonable for restructuring + likely soft guide in IT services with EU cloud-restriction headlines (per news) creating overhang on US IT services exposure to European clients.
entries.what_kills: If restructuring is targeted at low-margin contracts and signings/FCF guide raised, -11% is overdone

entries.name: Comstock Resources
entries.thesis: Earnings drop compounded by sector wipeout (XLE -4.1%) on oil pullback. CRK is a Haynesville natgas pure-play, so oil tape isn't the direct driver — earnings/guide must have disappointed on capex or production. Sector tape is amplifying.
entries.what_kills: If production beat and natgas peers also down 5%+, sympathy effect explains most of move

entries.name: Clean Harbors
entries.thesis: Clean earnings 8-K only. -8% with 4x volume on a +2.6% XLI day is meaningful underperformance. Likely guide miss on environmental services or PFAS-related cost commentary. Worth investigating because CLH typically trades stable.
entries.what_kills: If guide was reaffirmed and miss small, -8% is overdone for this quality compounder

entries.name: GEO Group
entries.thesis: Private prison/detention operator earnings beat in current administration's immigration-enforcement environment — operating leverage on detention bed contracts is the bull thesis. +21% suggests material guide raise on ICE contract expansion.
entries.what_kills: Beat driven by one-time items rather than contract expansion would mean rally fades

entries.name: Elanco Animal Health
entries.thesis: Earnings beat on a turnaround story — ELAN has been working through deleveraging and pet health product launches (Credelio Quattro, Zenrelia)
entries.what_kills: If guidance only nudged and beat was FX-driven, gains may fade

entries.name: California Resources
entries.thesis: Earnings combined with sector-wide energy selloff (XLE -4.1%) — double headwind from idiosyncratic print and sector
entries.what_kills: If XLE rebounds and CRC underperforms, idiosyncratic story is worse than appears; if CRC rebounds with sector, sympathy component dominated

entries.name: Freshpet
entries.thesis: Earnings disappointment continuing 5-day -17% slide — growth deceleration narrative for the high-multiple pet food story
entries.what_kills: If guidance was maintained and weakness is one-quarter weather/promotional, -17% 5-day is overdone
